tldr: Move mobile push and in-app messaging from Braze to Batch by replacing SDKs, re-importing user data, and rebuilding Canvases as Batch campaigns.
why_switch: Freemium entry pricing vs Braze's enterprise-tier pricing
Reduce lock-in to Braze's Canvas/Liquid model
Good fit for teams that don't need full omnichannel CRM
what_to_export: User profiles with stable external IDs
iOS and Android device tokens / platform metadata
export_method: Use Braze's user and event export capabilities documented in Braze's official docs to pull profiles, attributes, events, and subscription state. Capture Canvas flow logic and template content from the dashboard. Save exports in CSV/JSON for transformation before import.
import_method: Integrate Batch's iOS and Android SDKs following Batch's official integration docs, using your existing external IDs as Batch custom user IDs. Backfill custom attributes via Batch's documented APIs. Recreate segments, push templates, and in-app campaigns in the Batch dashboard, and rebuild Canvas flows as Batch triggered campaigns.

concept_mapping.note: Direct conceptual map; verify supported types
Multi-step Canvases require manual rebuild as chained triggers
Re-emit via Batch SDK / transactional API
Rewrite templates; syntax and helpers differ

gotchas: Push tokens are not portable; they're collected fresh by the Batch SDK after release
Liquid templating won't work in Batch — plan for a full template rewrite
Multi-step Canvas flows rarely map 1:1 to Batch triggered campaigns
Web push and email coverage in Batch is narrower than Braze's omnichannel
Subscription state must be migrated carefully to avoid messaging unsubscribed users
Batch pricing scales aggressively at high volumes — model costs before committing

email.subject: Braze → Batch migration plan
email.body:
Hi team,

We're planning to move our mobile push and in-app messaging from Braze to Batch to take advantage of EU data residency and a simpler, mobile-first product.

Approach:
1) Export users, devices, attributes, events, and subscription state from Braze.
2) Integrate Batch SDKs in iOS and Android using our existing external IDs.
3) Backfill attributes via Batch's APIs.
4) Rebuild segments, templates, and Canvas flows as Batch triggered campaigns.
5) Dual-run for one release cycle, then cut over.

Estimated effort: 4-10 weeks depending on Canvas complexity. Detailed checklist (templating rewrite, consent migration, omnichannel gaps) to follow.

Thanks
